{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Representative",
  "description": "Exhale Avenue Real Estate is a trusted and visionary property services company, proudly Nigerian and deeply committed to excellence. We specialize in providing tailored, value-driven real estate solutions for homeowners, investors, and developers alike.

Driven by our core values of integrity, client success, and inspired delivery, we go beyond selling properties, we guide you to profitable investments with 100% ROI in mind and

more. Over the years, we have expanded our services by leveraging our in-depth market expertise, reliable networks, and a passion for creating spaces that are both functional and fulfilling.

At Exhale Avenue, we don&rsquo;t just build houses, we build wealth, trust, and lasting impact.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Sales Representative / Marketer is responsible for generating leads, promoting the company&#39;sproducts and services, and converting prospects into clients.
	The role involves building and maintaining strong client relationships, conducting property presentations and inspections, negotiating sales, and achieving assigned sales targets while representing the company&#39;s brand with professionalism and integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and generate new sales leads through prospecting, referrals, networking, and marketing activities.
	Promote and market the company&#39;s real estate properties to prospective clients.
	Conduct property inspections and presentations for interested buyers and investors.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with existing and prospective clients.
	Follow up on inquiries and nurture leads through the sales pipeline.
	Prepare and submit sales reports, client feedback, and market intelligence to management.
	Achieve monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, or a related field.
	1 &ndash; 2 years of experience in sales, marketing, or real estate (real estate experience is an added advantage).
	Proven ability to meet or exceed sales targets.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong customer relationship management skills.
	Self-motivated, result-oriented, and able to work with minimal supervision.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and CRM tools is an added advantage.
	Strong networking and prospecting abilities.
	Professional appearance and presentation skills.


Renumerations


	Salary plus commissions on every sale.
	Performance based bonuses and incentives.


Method fo Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: info.exhaleavenue@gmail.com using &quot;Sales Representative&quot; as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Sales Representative Job at Exhale Avenue Real Estate




        
    

        
        Exhale Avenue Real Estate is a trusted and visionary property services company, proudly Nigerian and deeply committed to excellence. We specialize in providing tailored, value-driven real estate solutions for homeowners, investors, and developers alike.

Driven by our core values of integrity, client success, and inspired delivery, we go beyond selling properties, we guide you to profitable investments with 100% ROI in mind and

more. Over the years, we have expanded our services by leveraging our in-depth market expertise, reliable networks, and a passion for creating spaces that are both functional and fulfilling.

At Exhale Avenue, we don’t just build houses, we build wealth, trust, and lasting impact.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Sales Representative / Marketer is responsible for generating leads, promoting the company'sproducts and services, and converting prospects into clients.
	The role involves building and maintaining strong client relationships, conducting property presentations and inspections, negotiating sales, and achieving assigned sales targets while representing the company's brand with professionalism and integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and generate new sales leads through prospecting, referrals, networking, and marketing activities.
	Promote and market the company's real estate properties to prospective clients.
	Conduct property inspections and presentations for interested buyers and investors.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with existing and prospective clients.
	Follow up on inquiries and nurture leads through the sales pipeline.
	Prepare and submit sales reports, client feedback, and market intelligence to management.
	Achieve monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, or a related field.
	1 – 2 years of experience in sales, marketing, or real estate (real estate experience is an added advantage).
	Proven ability to meet or exceed sales targets.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong customer relationship management skills.
	Self-motivated, result-oriented, and able to work with minimal supervision.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and CRM tools is an added advantage.
	Strong networking and prospecting abilities.
	Professional appearance and presentation skills.


Renumerations


	Salary plus commissions on every sale.
	Performance based bonuses and incentives.


Method fo Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: [email protected] using "Sales Representative" as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR / Admin Officer",
  "description": "Temptimate Essentials Limited is a pharmaceutical company that manufactures and distributes supplements in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Officer

Job Location: Amuwo Odofin, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Workdays: Monday - Saturday

Job Description


	We are looking for an experienced HR and Admin Officer to manage human resources functions, ensure staff welfare, and maintain smooth office operations.


Core Responsibilities
Human Resources Management:


	Handle recruitment, onboarding, and staff records
	Manage payroll, leave, and disciplinary processes
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies


Administrative Support:


	Oversee office administration and general office operations
	Coordinate staff welfare, logistics, and vendor management
	Maintain office supplies and facility standards


Employee Relations:


	Serve as first point of contact for staff concerns
	Support staff engagement and workplace culture initiatives


Requirements


	HND / BSc Degree in HR, Business Administration, Law, or related field
	Minimum of 3 years of post-NYSC experience
	Must have completed NYSC
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices and Nigerian labor law


Added Advantage (Not Compulsory):


	Law background
	Experience in a pharmaceutical, FMCG, or manufacturing company.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / Month.&nbsp;

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV only to: mybetagift@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HR / Admin Officer Job at Temptimate Essentials Limited




        
    

        
        Temptimate Essentials Limited is a pharmaceutical company that manufactures and distributes supplements in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR / Admin Officer

Job Location: Amuwo Odofin, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Workdays: Monday - Saturday

Job Description


	We are looking for an experienced HR and Admin Officer to manage human resources functions, ensure staff welfare, and maintain smooth office operations.


Core Responsibilities
Human Resources Management:
     

            
    




	Handle recruitment, onboarding, and staff records
	Manage payroll, leave, and disciplinary processes
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies


Administrative Support:


	Oversee office administration and general office operations
	Coordinate staff welfare, logistics, and vendor management
	Maintain office supplies and facility standards


Employee Relations:


	Serve as first point of contact for staff concerns
	Support staff engagement and workplace culture initiatives


Requirements


	HND / BSc Degree in HR, Business Administration, Law, or related field
	Minimum of 3 years of post-NYSC experience
	Must have completed NYSC
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices and Nigerian labor law


Added Advantage (Not Compulsory):


	Law background
	Experience in a pharmaceutical, FMCG, or manufacturing company.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / Month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV only to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager",
  "description": "EHA Clinics, a subsidiary of eHealth Africa, is a health care network operating across Nigeria that provides high-quality, innovative, data-driven, and technology-enabled services. EHA Clinics delivers a comprehensive range of state of the art health care services in each location, e.g,&nbsp; general consultations, laboratory investigations, pharmaceuticals, annual medical checks, child wellness checks, ultrasonography, and specialized services (urgent care, telemedicine, home care, dental care, and ophthalmology).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Kano
Job Type: Full time

Mission Objective


	The REACH program is dedicated to closing healthcare gaps by bringing quality and affordable services directly to communities.&nbsp;
	The REACH Practice and Quality Assurance Coordinator is responsible for coordinating and overseeing daily administrative activities of the clinic to ensure compassionate, effective, and efficient delivery of high-quality care for patients. S/he is responsible for the supervision of all employed support staff.
	S/he is responsible to ensure the overall smooth day-to-day operations, and employee engagement.
	S/He is additionally responsible for assisting the REACH Program Manager to achieve departmental and organisational goals.


Duties and Responsibilities&nbsp; &nbsp;
Purpose:


	Provide expert, strategic advice, guidance and support to the REACH management team and other key stakeholders to ensure the design, development and implementation of high-quality, innovative partnership and relationship management strategies, systems, and processes.
	Contribute to specialised projects and undertake research to identify new and prospective partners across both the government and non-government sectors which maximise opportunities for shared value.


Engagement:


	Develop, build and maintain strong working relationships with key internal and external stakeholders through effective communication, negotiation, and issues management to foster collaboration, leverage expertise and maximise partnership opportunities.
	Provide specialist expertise and advice in team management, business, activity and performance planning and reporting to align partnership resources with key strategic objectives and priorities effectively.
	Manage the negotiation, development, and implementation of all relevant partnership agreements and documentation including Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs) and Service Level Agreements (SLAs), to ensure compliance with legislative, departmental and project requirements and to support the development of enduring and mutually beneficial partnerships.
	Organise community health events to promote our services and memberships.


Delivery:


	Identify, anticipate, and respond to current and future issues and priorities, and devise and recommend appropriate solutions to complex problems and issues, to mitigate risks and support continuous improvement across all partnership activities.
	Supervise hiring, supervision, and training of full-time and ad-hoc staff


Targeted Specialized Knowledge&nbsp;


	University Degree in the Social Sciences, Statistics, Business Administration, Economics, Public Health or another relevant field; a Master&rsquo;s degree is an advantage
	Minimum of 5 years of work experience with demonstrated results in project, program and / or portfolio management.
	Minimum 2 year of work experience with demonstrated results in data collection, data analysis, data visualization, survey design, statistics and / or field-level M&amp;E or quality assurance
	Advanced computer skills, particularly in Excel / Google Sheets, database and statistical applications
	Strong technical writing skills, including management and / or programmatic reports, project proposals, scientific manuscripts, SOPs and / or manuals;&nbsp;
	Demonstrated research and presentation skills; at least one presentation given at an external event (conference, panel, etc.);
	Experience designing and delivering training / workshops;&nbsp;
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, critical thinking, problem-solving, team working and team building skills; excellent organizational and time management skills.


Remuneration
N780,000 monthly gross.&nbsp;

Benefits:


	Health Coverage Benefit&nbsp;
	Performance Bonus
	13th Month
	Annual Skills Assessment.

Project Manager Job at EHA Clinics




        
    

        
        EHA Clinics, a subsidiary of eHealth Africa, is a health care network operating across Nigeria that provides high-quality, innovative, data-driven, and technology-enabled services. EHA Clinics delivers a comprehensive range of state of the art health care services in each location, e.g,  general consultations, laboratory investigations, pharmaceuticals, annual medical checks, child wellness checks, ultrasonography, and specialized services (urgent care, telemedicine, home care, dental care, and ophthalmology).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Kano
Job Type: Full time

Mission Objective
     

            
    




	The REACH program is dedicated to closing healthcare gaps by bringing quality and affordable services directly to communities. 
	The REACH Practice and Quality Assurance Coordinator is responsible for coordinating and overseeing daily administrative activities of the clinic to ensure compassionate, effective, and efficient delivery of high-quality care for patients. S/he is responsible for the supervision of all employed support staff.
	S/he is responsible to ensure the overall smooth day-to-day operations, and employee engagement.
	S/He is additionally responsible for assisting the REACH Program Manager to achieve departmental and organisational goals.


Duties and Responsibilities   
Purpose:


	Provide expert, strategic advice, guidance and support to the REACH management team and other key stakeholders to ensure the design, development and implementation of high-quality, innovative partnership and relationship management strategies, systems, and processes.
	Contribute to specialised projects and undertake research to identify new and prospective partners across both the government and non-government sectors which maximise opportunities for shared value.


Engagement:


	Develop, build and maintain strong working relationships with key internal and external stakeholders through effective communication, negotiation, and issues management to foster collaboration, leverage expertise and maximise partnership opportunities.
	Provide specialist expertise and advice in team management, business, activity and performance planning and reporting to align partnership resources with key strategic objectives and priorities effectively.
	Manage the negotiation, development, and implementation of all relevant partnership agreements and documentation including Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs) and Service Level Agreements (SLAs), to ensure compliance with legislative, departmental and project requirements and to support the development of enduring and mutually beneficial partnerships.
	Organise community health events to promote our services and memberships.


Delivery:


	Identify, anticipate, and respond to current and future issues and priorities, and devise and recommend appropriate solutions to complex problems and issues, to mitigate risks and support continuous improvement across all partnership activities.
	Supervise hiring, supervision, and training of full-time and ad-hoc staff


Targeted Specialized Knowledge 


	University Degree in the Social Sciences, Statistics, Business Administration, Economics, Public Health or another relevant field; a Master’s degree is an advantage
	Minimum of 5 years of work experience with demonstrated results in project, program and / or portfolio management.
	Minimum 2 year of work experience with demonstrated results in data collection, data analysis, data visualization, survey design, statistics and / or field-level M&E or quality assurance
	Advanced computer skills, particularly in Excel / Google Sheets, database and statistical applications
	Strong technical writing skills, including management and / or programmatic reports, project proposals, scientific manuscripts, SOPs and / or manuals; 
	Demonstrated research and presentation skills; at least one presentation given at an external event (conference, panel, etc.);
	Experience designing and delivering training / workshops; 
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, critical thinking, problem-solving, team working and team building skills; excellent organizational and time management skills.


Remuneration
N780,000 monthly gross. 

Benefits:


	Health Coverage Benefit 
	Performance Bonus
	13th Month
	Annual Skills Assessment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Data Analyst",
  "description": "Mwanga is a pioneer tech-driven debt recovery and business support outsourcing company with a vision to be the leading extension to global teams in Africa. We started our pilot operations in 2018 with a small agile team and have scaled impressively since then in results and team becoming trusted by key players for recoveries in Nigeria. We offer business support to businesses with the aim of helping them scale using ethical and innovative technology strategies. We are trusted by Africa&rsquo;s leading lenders to recover their outstanding debts and to scale our impact. We provide a range of services ranging from debt recovery, telemarketing, call center outsourcing, virtual assistant services, customer support services, and data analysis.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Data Analyst

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry:Business Process Outsourcing (BPO)

Job Summary


	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Junior Data Analyst to support business operations by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to generate actionable insights.
	The role will also support workforce management activities, including scheduling, performance tracking, and operational reporting to ensure efficiency and optimal resource utilization within a 24/7 BPO environment.
	The successful candidate will receive onboarding and guidance on workforce management processes within the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Collect, clean, and organize data from multiple sources, including CRM systems, call logs, and operational tools
	Analyze operational performance data such as customer engagement, recovery rates, and service levels
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards
	Track and report key performance indicators (KPIs) across teams
	Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in business data
	Support decision-making through data-driven insights
	Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity


Workforce Management (WFM) Responsibilities:


	Support staff scheduling and shift planning based on workload forecasts
	Monitor agent availability, productivity, and schedule adherence
	Generate workforce performance reports, including attendance, utilization, and efficiency metrics
	Analyze call volumes and workload trends to support staffing decisions
	Provide insights to improve workforce efficiency and service levels


Requirements / Skills / Qualifications


	0&ndash;2 years of relevant experience
	Experience with reporting or data analysis is an advantage
	Exposure to call center/BPO operations is a plus
	Basic understanding of workforce management concepts is an added advantage
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets (advanced Excel preferred)
	Ability to adapt in a fast-paced, 24/7 operational environment
	Knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, or other data visualization tools
	Basic SQL knowledge is an advantage
	Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
	Strong attention to detail and reporting/documentation skills
	Good communication and computer literacy skills


Salary
N120,000 - 150,000 Net / Monthly.",

Junior Data Analyst Job at Mwanga Limited




        
    

        
        Mwanga is a pioneer tech-driven debt recovery and business support outsourcing company with a vision to be the leading extension to global teams in Africa. We started our pilot operations in 2018 with a small agile team and have scaled impressively since then in results and team becoming trusted by key players for recoveries in Nigeria. We offer business support to businesses with the aim of helping them scale using ethical and innovative technology strategies. We are trusted by Africa’s leading lenders to recover their outstanding debts and to scale our impact. We provide a range of services ranging from debt recovery, telemarketing, call center outsourcing, virtual assistant services, customer support services, and data analysis.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Data Analyst

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry:Business Process Outsourcing (BPO)

Job Summary


	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Junior Data Analyst to support business operations by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to generate actionable insights.
	The role will also support workforce management activities, including scheduling, performance tracking, and operational reporting to ensure efficiency and optimal resource utilization within a 24/7 BPO environment.
	The successful candidate will receive onboarding and guidance on workforce management processes within the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Collect, clean, and organize data from multiple sources, including CRM systems, call logs, and operational tools
	Analyze operational performance data such as customer engagement, recovery rates, and service levels
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards
	Track and report key performance indicators (KPIs) across teams
	Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in business data
	Support decision-making through data-driven insights
	Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity


Workforce Management (WFM) Responsibilities:


	Support staff scheduling and shift planning based on workload forecasts
	Monitor agent availability, productivity, and schedule adherence
	Generate workforce performance reports, including attendance, utilization, and efficiency metrics
	Analyze call volumes and workload trends to support staffing decisions
	Provide insights to improve workforce efficiency and service levels


Requirements / Skills / Qualifications


	0–2 years of relevant experience
	Experience with reporting or data analysis is an advantage
	Exposure to call center/BPO operations is a plus
	Basic understanding of workforce management concepts is an added advantage
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets (advanced Excel preferred)
	Ability to adapt in a fast-paced, 24/7 operational environment
	Knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, or other data visualization tools
	Basic SQL knowledge is an advantage
	Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
	Strong attention to detail and reporting/documentation skills
	Good communication and computer literacy skills


Salary
N120,000 - 150,000 Net / Monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Front Desk / Customer Service Personnel Job at Voedsel Capital Limited




        
    

        
        Voedsel Capital Limited is a team of young, dynamic and goal-driven individuals, who have a common goal to improve the lives of our clients through access to Finance. Voedsel Capital Limited is focused on improving the productivity of Micro, Small and Medium-scale businesses that have displayed evidence of traction and possess huge growth potential but do not have access to traditional financing solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk / Customer Service Personnel

Job Location: Kado, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a smart, professional, and customer-focused Female Front Desk/Customer Service Officer to join our team.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to clients and visitors in a warm and professional manner.
	Manage the front desk and ensure a positive customer experience at all times.
	Answer, screen, and direct phone calls and respond to customer enquiries promptly.
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence.
	Schedule appointments and coordinate meetings where necessary.
	Maintain a clean, organized, and professional reception area.
	Provide administrative support to various departments as required.
	Resolve customer complaints or escalate issues appropriately while ensuring excellent service delivery.


Requirements


	Must have completed the NYSC programme.
	Minimum of 1–2 years of experience in a Front Desk, Receptionist, or Customer Service role.
	Bachelor's degree or HND in a relevant field.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong interpersonal and customer service skills.
	Good organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook).
	Professional appearance and a positive attitude.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: If you meet the above requirements and are passionate about delivering exceptional customer service, please apply.    

    

        Click here to Apply
